steps.title: Understand what infinitely many solutions means
steps.content: An equation has infinitely many solutions when it is true for all values of the variable. This happens only when both sides of the equation are identical expressions.

steps.title: Apply this to the equation
steps.content:
The given equation is
$$6x + k = 6x + 5.$$
Both sides already have the term $6x$. For the sides to be identical for all values of $x$, the constants must be equal, so $k = 5$.
